items.name: ggsql
items.source_story_title: ggsql: A Grammar of Graphics for SQL
items.summary: A SQL-based visualization DSL that commenters found compelling and worth trying.
items.why_included: Multiple commenters gave direct positive reactions and said they would try it.
items.evidence: Commenters said ggsql looked awesome, liked it, and would try it soon.

items.name: GLM-5.1
items.summary: A low-cost coding model family praised for strong results and good price/performance.
items.why_included: Clear praise for coding quality and value, with concrete first-hand use.
items.evidence: One commenter said GLM-5 is really good, especially on pricing, and another used GLM-5.1 with Claude Code.

items.name: MiniMax M2.5
items.summary: A budget-friendly coding model that one commenter says they rely on heavily.
items.why_included: Strong positive endorsement with specific usage and pricing details.
items.evidence: A commenter said they get all their coding done with MiniMax M2.5 in parallel sessions for $10/month.

items.name: Flatpak
items.source_story_title: OpenClaw isn't fooling me. I remember MS-DOS
items.summary: Linux app sandboxing and packaging system.
items.why_included: Direct preference for a specific Linux app distribution and sandboxing solution.
items.evidence: A commenter preferred the Flatpak solution because it gives very detailed, fine-grained control.

items.name: IR sauna bags
items.why_included: Concrete product class explicitly described as working well as a sauna substitute.
items.evidence: A commenter said IR sauna bags you can buy online work great and give the same effects as a sauna.

items.name: Calibre
items.summary: Open-source ebook library manager and format converter.
items.why_included: Clear positive endorsement of a concrete ebook tool.
items.evidence: A comment called Calibre the escape hatch and the best tool for getting a library out of Amazon's format.

items.name: Kobo
items.summary: Alternative e-reader ecosystem with broad reader support.
items.why_included: Explicit positive switch away from Kindle to Kobo.
items.evidence: A user said they switched to the Kobo ecosystem and have been pretty happy.

items.name: KOReader
items.summary: Customizable ebook reader app for dedicated e-readers.
items.why_included: Positive report on a concrete alternative reading app.
items.evidence: A user said they use KOReader and the UI is now stable and tailored to their taste.

items.name: Libby
items.summary: Library borrowing app for instant ebook checkout.
items.why_included: Strong positive recommendation of a library-borrowing app.
items.evidence: A user said Libby hooks into many US library systems and feels hugely convenient and magical.

items.name: BookShelves
items.summary: EPUB and comics reader with Calibre wireless sync.
items.why_included: Concrete app recommendation with clear positive use.
items.evidence: A user said they ended up on BookShelves after trying a few options; it reads EPUBs and comics and has Calibre sync.

items.name: xteink X4
items.summary: Pocketable e-ink reader praised for portability.
items.why_included: Direct praise for a specific e-ink device.
items.evidence: A user said they bought the xteink X4 and called it a great little device.
